title: Valerianoides sanguinea
intro: Valerianoides sanguinea, also known as Sanguinea, is a flowering plant species within the family Valerianaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Valerianoides sanguinea include valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Valerianoides sanguinea is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat various health issues.
taxonomy: Valerianoides sanguinea is a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae, commonly known as the Honeysuckle family. It belongs to the genus Valerianoides, which is a group of plants closely related to the valerian family. The species is native to certain regions, specifically in the Americas. Further classification may include the order Dipsacales and the clade Asterids. As a member of the Caprifoliaceae family, Valerianoides sanguinea shares characteristics with other plants in this family.
